chapter_title.en: Chapter on Emancipation and Its Rulings

text: 3442 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ تَكُونُ لَهُ اَلْأَمَةُ فَيَقُولُ مَتَى آتِيهَا فَهِيَ حُرَّةٌ ثُمَّ يَبِيعُهَا مِنْ رَجُلٍ ثُمَّ يَشْتَرِيهَا بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ قَالَ "لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ يَأْتِيَهَا قَدْ خَرَجَتْ مِنْ مِلْكِهِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3442 - Al-Ala' narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from one of the Imams, peace be upon them, who said:
I asked him about a man who owned a bondwoman and said: "Whenever I approach her, she will be free." Then he sold her to another man and later bought her back.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in approaching her, as she had exited his ownership."
